---
title: "Sales Dashboard"
code-fold: true
---

## SME Annual Financial Overview

This dashboard presents a financial overview of a small-to-medium enterprise (SME),
analysing monthly revenue, costs and profit across a full calendar year. Built using
Python and Matplotlib. This dashboard is is similar to those that we have prodcued as 
part of the Applied Consultancy Project. (For data protection reasons, the data repsented here is fictional)

```{python}
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from plotly.subplots import make_subplots

months = ["Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun",
          "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"]

revenue = [42, 58, 55, 70, 65, 80, 78, 90, 85, 95, 102, 120]
costs   = [30, 35, 33, 40, 38, 45, 44, 50, 47, 52,  55,  60]
profit  = [r - c for r, c in zip(revenue, costs)]

fig = make_subplots(
    rows=1, cols=2,
    subplot_titles=("Revenue vs Costs (€k)", "Monthly Profit (€k)")
)

# Revenue line
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=months, y=revenue,
    mode="lines+markers",
    name="Revenue",
    line=dict(color="#1B2A4A", width=2.5),
    marker=dict(size=7)
), row=1, col=1)

# Costs line
fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
    x=months, y=costs,
    mode="lines+markers",
    name="Costs",
    line=dict(color="#C9A84C", width=2.5),
    marker=dict(size=7)
), row=1, col=1)

# Profit bars
bar_colors = ["#1B2A4A" if p >= 0 else "#cc3333" for p in profit]
fig.add_trace(go.Bar(
    x=months, y=profit,
    name="Profit",
    marker_color=bar_colors,
    width=0.6
), row=1, col=2)

fig.update_layout(
    title=dict(text="SME Annual Financial Overview", font=dict(size=15, color="#1B2A4A")),
    plot_bgcolor="#F4F6FA",
    paper_bgcolor="#F4F6FA",
    font=dict(color="#1B2A4A"),
    legend=dict(orientation="h", y=-0.15),
    height=450
)

fig.update_yaxes(title_text="€ (thousands)", gridcolor="#dddddd", row=1, col=1)
fig.update_yaxes(title_text="€ (thousands)", gridcolor="#dddddd", row=1, col=2)

fig.show()
```

## Key Takeaways

- **Revenue** grew steadily across the year, peaking at **€120k** in December
- **Costs** remained controlled, never exceeding 50% of revenue
- **Profit** was positive every month, reaching **€60k** in December
